ex.en: He rode the lift up and skied down.
ex.ko: 그는 리프트를 타고 올라가서 스키를 타고 내려왔다.

ex.en: She loves watching Korean dramas.
ex.ko: 그녀는 한국 드라마 보는 것을 아주 좋아한다.

ex.en: She can draw a beautiful picture.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아름다운 그림을 그릴 수 있다.

ex.en: I had a dream about you last night.
She wore a beautiful dress to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 아름다운 드레스를 입고 갔다.

ex.en: He drank a glass of water.
She drives to work every morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 아침 차를 몰고 출근한다.

ex.en: The bus driver was very friendly.
Don't drop the dishes.
He plays the drum in a band.
The desert air is very dry.
The rent is due on the first of the month.
We watched a movie on DVD.
They received ten dollars each.

enOverride: used to say that two or more people do the same thing to one another

ex.en: They love each other very much.
She whispered something in his ear.
He always wakes up early.
The test was very easy.
She was eating a banana.
He fried an egg for breakfast.
She has eight cousins.
He turned eighteen last month.
My grandmother is eighty years old.
He isn't stupid, but he isn't a genius either.
My sister goes to elementary school.
Class starts at eleven o'clock.

enOverride: used to refer to something in addition, or something different

ex.en: Would you like anything else?
Please send me an email.
We reached the end of the road.
He works as an engineer at a tech company.
She enjoys reading books.
He enjoyed the evening light across the lake.
ex.ko: 그는 호수를 가로지르는 저녁 빛을 즐겼다.

ex.en: The wedding was a big event for the family.
Every person is different.
Everybody enjoyed the party.
Cooking is part of my everyday life.
Everyone was happy with the result.

enOverride: all things; the whole of something

ex.en: She lost everything in the fire.
You can find fast food stores everywhere.
Can you give me an example?
She did an excellent job.
The kids were excited about the trip.
She told an exciting story about her trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행에 대한 흥미진진한 이야기를 들려줬다.

ex.en: He kept finding excuses to stay.
ex.ko: 그는 계속 남아 있을 핑계를 찾았다.

ex.en: She exercises every morning.
This restaurant is very expensive.
She has beautiful blue eyes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아름다운 파란 눈을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: He washed his face.
He works at a car factory.
The judge was fair to both sides.
The story is about a little fairy.
ex.ko: 그 이야기는 작은 요정에 관한 것이다.

ex.en: He gave false testimony under oath.
ex.ko: 그는 선서를 하고도 거짓 증언을 했다.

ex.en: He moved his family to a new city.
She is a famous actor.
He is a big fan of that singer.
It takes several people to work the farm.
ex.ko: 그 농장을 운영하려면 여러 사람이 필요하다.

ex.en: The farmer grows corn and wheat.
How fast can he get here?
ex.ko: 그가 얼마나 빨리 여기 올 수 있나요?

ex.en: The cat got fat from eating too much.
ex.ko: 그 고양이는 너무 많이 먹어서 살이 쪘다.

ex.en: His father was born in Atlanta.
Pizza is my favorite food.
Valentine's Day is in February.
She feeds the cat every morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매일 아침 고양이에게 먹이를 준다.

ex.en: She felt resentful about the decision.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 결정에 대해 억울함을 느꼈다.

ex.en: She had a feeling of joy.
They enjoyed the music festival.

He planted a field of wheat.

He runs fifty kilometers a week.
The two boys got into a fight.

He filled the glass with water.
We find this idea in many old books.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 생각을 여러 옛 책에서 찾을 수 있다.

ex.en: The passengers were shaken up but are all fine.
He finished the dishes.
They lost everything in the fire.
First, we must consider the plan.
ex.ko: 먼저, 우리는 그 계획을 고려해야 한다.

ex.en: The shark is a large fish.
He goes fishing every weekend.

ex.ko: 그녀는 남자 형제가 다섯 명 있다.

ex.en: The flag waved in the wind.
The ground here is flat.

She grows flowers in her garden.
Birds can fly high in the sky.
ex.ko: 새는 하늘 높이 날 수 있다.

ex.en: Please focus on your studies.

enOverride: covered or filled with fog, making it hard to see

ex.en: It was a foggy morning.
Please answer the following questions.
She cooked delicious food for us.
ex.ko: 그녀는 우리를 위해 맛있는 음식을 만들었다.

ex.en: They play football every Saturday.

enOverride: used to show the purpose, destination, or duration of something

ex.en: This gift is for you.
She speaks three foreign languages.
Many foreigners visit Seoul every year.
Don't forget to call me.
Please fill out this form.
My uncle is forty years old.
She has four cats.

The tickets are free for children.
We usually go out on Friday nights.
He was my best friend at university.
ex.ko: 그는 대학 시절 나의 가장 친한 친구였다.

ex.en: A frog jumped into the pond.

enOverride: used to show the starting point, source, or origin of something

ex.en: She is from Canada.
He sat in the front row.
She eats fruit every morning.
The sky was full of stars.
We had a lot of fun at the beach.
That was a really funny movie.
She has big plans for the future.
ex.ko: 그녀는 미래를 위한 큰 계획이 있다.

ex.en: You need four people to play this game.
ex.ko: 이 게임을 하려면 네 명이 필요하다.

ex.en: Please take out the garbage.

He looked like he had seen a ghost.

ex.en: She gave him a birthday gift.

ex.en: Let's go to the park.
